title: Secure Protocols, State Machines, Replay, and Downgrade · Runtime Contract: Crypto Go Replay Duplicate Policy
summary: A device pairing protocol encrypts messages but has no explicit roles, transcript, sequence numbers, version negotiation, terminal states, or duplicate policy. Predict the asset, adversary, security property, trust boundary, and falsifying evidence before opening the governing standard. Planned artifact: Add and verify the replay duplicate policy increment in authenticated protocol state machine. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and…
objectives: Use nonces, counters, windows, durable identities, expiry, and idempotent effects according to channel and availability constraints.
Bind ordered message type, role, version, suite, identities, ephemeral values, session ID, sequence, and prior transcript into each state transition.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Cryptography and Go Predict Crypto Go Replay Duplicate Policy · Theory Crypto Go Replay Duplicate P…
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory cryptographic engineering team handles case 8919 during a backup restoration drill. Build deterministic Go evidence to advance a role-bound protocol state with session identity, ordered replay defense, transcript binding, and downgrade rejection; keep browser execution deterministic and move production entropy, native timing, non-allowlisted crypto, network, HSM, and compliance claims to authorized transfer gates; then double attacker-controlled size or work and remain inside the admission budget. Browser code uses pure state models or the narrow side-effect-free crypto allowlist with fixed fixtures. Production entropy and keys, secret custody, native timing and side channels, networks, TLS, HSM/KMS, certificate systems, FIPS or compliance state, load, faults, restore, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Crypto Go Replay Duplicate Policy contract for the supplied data.
